spec_match.rationale: Listens for native `wheel` events on a live preview tile and forwards them via `controller.forwardWheel(event)` to the captured tab. That is the spec's flagship motivating recipe — no synthetic scroll-button widgets.

interactive.rationale: Real preview tile wired to live wheel events with 3 listeners, action log for each forwarded delta, and a feature-detect probe.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises `wheel on the preview tile, the DOM wheel event is translated and forwarded via controller.forwardWheel() — no synthetic buttons`. The page is exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Real wheel events work but the page doesn't show what happens when the wheel event would scroll past the captured tab's bounds, when the source has been paused/ended, or when `forwardWheel` is called before any wheel arrives (no-op vs error).

references.rationale: `see also` cites the chromestatus link and the WICG spec.

design_system.rationale: 53 CSS variable references, no raw hex, no button-color/background mismatch.

openQuestions.title: Add the origin-trial banner
openQuestions.detail: Captured Surface Control is OT-only at v136. The page should display a `enable chrome://flags/#captured-surface-control or join the origin trial` banner per CLAUDE.md's behind-a-flag concept policy.

openQuestions.title: Surface the trackended / source-paused case
openQuestions.detail: If the user clicks `stop sharing` from the Chrome control bar mid-session, the captured stream ends but the page is still wired to forward wheel events. Listen for `track.onended` and gracefully tear down the wheel listener so the action log can show `source ended` rather than silently failing.

openQuestions.title: Show wheel delta clamping / translation
openQuestions.detail: The spec lets implementations clamp huge deltas. Try a programmatic `dispatchEvent(new WheelEvent('wheel', {deltaY: 1e6}))` and surface what value actually reaches the captured tab — that round-trip is the only way to know what the browser is doing under the hood.

summary: Right-shaped concept page for the spec's motivating wheel-passthrough use case with real event forwarding, a working preview tile, and full references. Missing the OT-banner that the behind-a-flag rules in CLAUDE.md require, and the stream-ended cleanup path; both would round out the failure-mode coverage.
